source: A Guide to World Services in Narcotics Anonymous, 2023–2026 Conference Cycle

glossary.definition: World Service Conference — the decision-making body for NA World Services, meeting biennially with regional and zonal delegates.
The board of NA World Services, Inc. Manages all World Services activities including oversight of the World Service Office. Members serve up to two consecutive 6-year terms.
World Service Office — NA's main service center in Chatsworth, CA. Maintains correspondence, prints/distributes literature, and maintains NA archives.
Primary contact between World Services and a local NA community. Attends WSC and virtual conference participant meetings, has one vote.
Represents a zone (group of regions) at the WSC. Zones with two or more zonally seated regions may send a zonal delegate.
Human Resource Panel — facilitates election/selection processes for WSC trusted servant positions. Two elected members serve 6-year terms.
Preside over CAR- and CAT-related Discussions and Decisions during the WSC. Two elected members serve 6-year terms.
Conference Agenda Report — published before each WSC containing motions for fellowship review and regional input. Includes World Board motions and regional/zonal motions.
Conference Approval Track — material published for approval at the WSC including budget, project plans, seating requests, service material, and policy changes.

Consensus-Based Decision Making — the WSC's decision-making process. Based on respect for all participants, seeking group consent rather than simple majority. Uses straw polls and discussion to build consensus before formal votes.
Fellowship Intellectual Property Trust — governs NA's name, trademarks, and recovery literature. NA World Services serves as Trustee; the fellowship (through delegates) is the Beneficiary.
Narcotics Anonymous World Services, Inc. — the legal entity that serves as Trustee of the FIPT and operates the WSO.
An informal measure of where the body stands on an issue. Not a binding decision. Often taken multiple times during discussion to gauge movement.
Any delegate (RD or ZD), World Board member, or other seated participant at the WSC. Each has one vote.
The period between WSC meetings, typically two years. The current cycle is 2023–2026.
Issue Discussion Topics — fellowship-wide discussion topics selected at the WSC for exploration during the conference cycle.
A proposal submitted by any conference participant for discussion at the WSC. Uses a standard form with maker, topic, proposal text, and supporting material.
The closing session of the WSC where decisions are made about issue discussion topics, workgroups, ongoing processes, and other matters for the cycle ahead.

consensus_thresholds.description: Motion passes. If initial straw poll shows consensus (80%+), Cofacilitators announce as final decision (2025-2026 only).
Motion passes (meets 2/3 majority threshold required for adoption).
Motion fails. Does not meet the 2/3 majority threshold.
Body has reached consensus against the motion.
No voting participants support the motion.

decision_process: 1. Initial straw poll conducted via epoll before WSC meeting to measure support.
2. If initial straw poll shows consensus (80%+ support or not in support), Cofacilitators announce as final decision.
3. If 20-80% support, amendments are considered first — each amendment gets its own straw poll and discussion.
4. Facilitator manages discussion using an unordered discussion pool, calling on members in turn (2 min per speaker).
5. Intermediate straw polls taken during discussion to measure movement.
6. Before final vote, all participants asked if they are ready to decide.
7. Final vote taken — 2/3 majority (66.67%) of those present and voting required to pass.
8. For FIPT matters, only RDs and ZDs may vote, requiring 2/3 of those recorded at most recent roll call.
9. Adopted motions take effect at the conclusion of the conference unless otherwise stated.

vote_types.description: Participants verbally indicate yes or no. Used when outcome appears clear.
Participants stand or raise hands to be counted. Used when voice vote is unclear.
Any member can request. Facilitator calls each participant's name; they respond yes, no, abstain, or present not voting.
Electronic poll sent to all seated participants. Can be used for decisions outside of the WSC meeting.
Used for elections. HRP facilitates distribution, collection, and tabulation.

key_rules: Each conference participant has only one vote.
No member may speak on a motion more than once until others who wish to speak have had a chance.
Members may speak for a maximum of 2 minutes each time recognized.
A quorum is a majority of all eligible voting members.
Abstentions are effectively counted as not supporting the motion.
Present but not voting is different from abstention — not counted in the vote total.
Amendments must be submitted 60 days before WSC opening (45 days for interim).
An amendment shall not replace a motion or introduce a substitute or contrary motion.
Regional and zonal motions must be received by July 1 and be in CAR-ready form by August 3.

service_structure.description: The foundation of the NA service structure. Any group of addicts who meet regularly for the purpose of recovery.
Serves the needs of groups in a defined geographic area.
Optional body that coordinates multiple areas in a metropolitan area.
Serves the needs of areas and groups in a defined region. Elects the Regional Delegate.
Communication and support body for multiple regions. May send Zonal Delegate to WSC.
The decision-making body for NA World Services. Meets biennially.
Manages all World Services activities between conferences.
NA's primary service center, carrying out WSC directives.
